name: Raspberry Mousse
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Raspberries, fresh OR 30 oz Raspberries, frozen, thawed, and drained (liquid reserved)
recipeInstructions:
1. Choose a few perfect raspberries for garnish and set aside.
2. Puree the remaining raspberries in a processor or blender until very smooth. Transfer 2 tablespoons of puree (with seeds) to a measuring cup. Strain the remaining puree into a mixing bowl, removing all seeds. Add enough strained puree to the 2 tablespoons to measure 1/2 cup. Set aside and chill.
3. In a small heavy saucepan, combine sugar, water, and corn syrup. Bring slowly to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally and brushing any sugar crystals from the sides of the pan with a cold water-dipped brush. Raise heat slightly and cook until the temperature reaches 240°F (soft ball stage).
4. As soon as the syrup reaches 240°F, lower the mixer speed to slow and pour the hot syrup onto the beaten egg whites in a thin stream.
5. When all of the syrup has been incorporated, raise the mixer speed and beat the meringue until cool.
6. Stir together the reserved raspberry puree and lemon juice and fold into the meringue until nearly blended.
7. Whip the cream until stiff and fold into the meringue until blended. Do not overmix.
